titleStmt.title: A Transcription of John in the Wolfenbüttel Lectionary Palimpsest
VL32
The New Testament
The Gospel according to John
john
titleStmt.funder: The Arts and Humanities Research Council

sponsor.name: The Institute for Textual Scholarship and Electronic Editing

publisher.name: The Institute for Textual Scholarship and Electronic Editing
University of Birmingham

availability.p: Licensed under a Creative Commons Attribution 4.0 International License (CC BY 4.0): available for re-use provided attribution is made to the original creators (the IGNTP and any named participants) and an indication is given of any changes made. See further .

surrogates.bibl: A. Dold, Das älteste Liturgiebuch der lateinischen Kirche. Ein altgallikanisches Lektionar des 5./6. Jhs aus dem Wolfenbütteler Palimpsest-Codex Weissenburgensis 76. (Texte und Arbeiten 26-28) Beuron 1936.

projectDesc.p: This transcription was made by the Verbum Project as part of an online and printed edition of the Old Latin versions of John between 2003 and 2010.

editorialDecl.p: This transcription only includes texts from John: some of these are complete lections, whereas others are individual verses extracted from readings composed of verses from all four Gospels. The pagination follows Dold's reconstruction of the quires of the primary text: the current pagination of the manuscript is provided in a note at the top of each page.The text was transcribed from Dold's edition. Text italicized by Dold has been tagged as reconstruction. Dold's punctuation and capitalisation have been reproduced exactly. Letters in smaller type (usually final 'm') have been treated as abbreviations.The initial transcription file was a plain text file for use with COLLATE. Further information about the procedures followed may be found in the electronic edition at
This second edition of the file was converted to XML by Catherine Smith using the project's Python conversion scripts 1.0.
The XML specifications used are version 1.5 of the IGNTP Guidelines available at

revisionDesc.change: Adjustment of transcription to version 1.5 of the XML by Hugh Houghton.
Reconversion of the original plain text file using Python scripts to version 1.4 of the XML by Catherine Smith and manual checking.
Hyphen entity replaced by lb attribute break="no" by Hugh Houghton
Changes made in order to display properly with VLI.xsl transformation by Hugh Houghton
Transcription converted to the TEI XML schema and corrected for parsing by Hugh Houghton

ab.note: The first half of this page is a reading from Acts 13:41-48.
The m is printed in smaller type, so is presumably an abbreviation
There is a gap of 10 lines before the text resumes part way through a lection from Isaiah.
The first part of the page is the conclusion of a lection from Acts 4.
After a gap of 2 lines, there is a lection from James 1:2-7.
The lection on the next page, fol 19v, is insufficiently legible to be identified.
This page consists of a lection mixed from all four gospels. Only the Johannine lections have been transcribed.
There is no more John on this page.
The first lection on this page is from Matt 20:14-16 and Matt 15:29-35. The Johannine material begins without a new introduction.
A lection from Matthew 18:23-34 begins on the next line.
The first four lines of this page consist of a reading from Luke 22:31-33, which leads into the Johannine verses without a break.
The first four lines of this page consist of a lection from Mt 3:16-17, which leads into the Johannine verses without a break.
The lection continues with Matthew 15:29-31, and then John 6:1-2, 5-14.
The first four lines of this page consist of a lection from Mt 15:30-31, which leads into the Johannine verses without a break.
Little is legible of the first 20 lines of this page.
Note that this passage also appears on fol. 73v.
Dold is unsure about the presence of 'ait ih¬s petro'.
The rest of the line is Luke 10:23, 'et conuersus ad discipulos suos'.
This page consists of a lection mixed from Matthew, Luke and John. Only the Johannine verses have been transcribed.
The first part of the page is Matthew 27:24-34. The Johannine verses follow without a break.
Dold's edition reads 'patres', but as he usually marks misspellings with an exclamation mark (!), this has been taken to be a typographical error.
The first lines of this page consist of Matthew 27:53-54.
The first part of the page is a lection from 1 and 2 Timothy. Note that the lection from John 21 also appears on fol. 67r.
As on fol. 67r, the lection continues with Luke 10:23, 'et conuersus ad discipulos suos'.
The lection which begins this page is from Hebrews 13.
Dold's edition reads 'andiunt' for 'audiunt', but as he usually marks misspellings with an exclamation mark (!), this has been taken to be a typographical error.
The '-mus' of 'sumus' is written on the line below. After a line from Lev 2:2, a reading follows from Isaiah 40.
On this line is the reading 'iustus maiore'. A lection from Isaiah 40 begins on the bottom line.
The first 9 lines of the page are a lection from 1 Cor. 15:54-58. There is then a blank line.
After a blank line, there is a lection from Isaiah, and then from 1 Corinthians 7.
